# Generative AI in Academic Research: Database on Usage, Perception and Productivity

> Structured database on the use of generative artificial intelligence (GAI) in academic research and university teaching in Latin America (2022–2025).

## Author
Juan Moisés de la Serna Tuya (UNIR)
ORCID: 0000-0002-8401-8018
Email: jmsernatuya@gmail.com

## Description
Comprehensive structured database documenting the adoption, perception, and productivity impacts of generative AI tools (ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, etc.) among students, researchers, and faculty in Latin American universities. Covers frequency of use, application areas (writing, data analysis, coding, literature review), perceived benefits and risks, ethical concerns, and effects on academic productivity. Data collected 2022–2025.

## Coverage
- Population: Students, researchers, and faculty at Latin American universities
- Countries: Mexico, Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Chile, Peru, and others
- Years: 2022–2025
- Variables: GAI tool usage frequency, application domain, productivity self-assessment, ethical concerns, disciplinary field, academic level

## Key Topics
- ChatGPT usage in academic writing
- AI-assisted literature review
- Code generation in research
- Ethical concerns about AI in education
- Productivity impacts of generative AI
- Disciplinary differences in AI adoption

## Dashboard URL
https://juanmoisesd.github.io/generative-ai-in-academic-research-database-on-usage-perception-and-productivity/

## Keywords
generative AI, ChatGPT, academic research, university teaching, latin america, productivity, AI ethics, higher education, open data
